rows.text: x402 is an open protocol for HTTP-native payments: a server answers 402 Payment Required with machine-readable requirements and the client retries with a signed payment.
EIP-3009 transferWithAuthorization lets a USDC holder sign a transfer off-chain; anyone can broadcast it, so the payer needs no ETH for gas.
A facilitator verifies signed payment authorizations and settles them on-chain. It never holds customer funds; the authorization is bound to amount, recipient, and validity window.
Sluice is a pay-per-request gateway: it prices routes from a rules table and forwards to the origin only after payment settles.
USDC on Base uses six decimal places, so 10000 atomic units equal one cent.
Full-text search ranks documents with BM25; hybrid search fuses BM25 and vector similarity using reciprocal rank fusion.
An API gateway terminates client connections, applies policy such as pricing or quotas, and reverse-proxies requests to backend origins.
Anvil can fork a live chain so contracts like USDC run locally against real state with fake value.
